      "class_name": "Class 9",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U1",
      "unit_name": "Matter: Its Nature and Behaviour (Implicit from context of Lavoisier, Dalton)",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Contributions of Antoine Lavoisier, Joseph Proust, and John Dalton",
        "Handling common laboratory chemicals and apparatus safely",
        "Electron dot structures of atoms and molecules",
        "Applications of atoms and molecules (medicine, energy, peaceful use)",
        "Atomic bonding to social bonding",
        "Educational games for chemical formulae"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Indian scientists in atomic energy",
        "Traditional use of cinnabar pigment"
      ],
      "key_concepts": []
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Class 9",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U2",
      "unit_name": "Earth as a System: Energy, Matter & Life",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Interconnected system spheres of the Earth (biosphere, geosphere, hydrosphere, cryosphere and atmosphere)",
        "Nature of solar energy: radiation, electromagnetic spectrum, speed of light",
        "Solar energy interaction with the Earth's Surface and differential heating",
        "Biogeochemical cycles (water cycle, carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, oxygen cycle)",
        "Human impact on Earth's system"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Latitude and tilt of the Earth causing differential heating",
        "Role of atmosphere in weather and climate",
        "Reflectivity of different materials",
        "Recycling of elements (carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, water) between biotic and abiotic environments"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Earth as interconnected system",
        "Nature of solar energy: electromagnetic waves having different frequencies",
        "Differential heating causes winds",
        "Mountain, valley, sea and land breezes"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Class 9",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U3",
      "unit_name": "Motion",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Displacement, velocity, acceleration",
        "Graphical representation of motion (position-time and velocity-time graphs)",
        "Kinematic equations for motion in a straight line with constant acceleration",
        "Uniform circular motion"
      ],
      "subtopics": [],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Motion — displacement, velocity, acceleration",
        "Graphical representation of motion for an object moving in a straight line (constant velocity and constant acceleration)",
        "Elementary idea of uniform circular motion"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Class 9",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U4",
      "unit_name": "Force and Laws of Motion",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Force; balanced and unbalanced forces",
        "Force of friction",
        "Newton's first law of motion",
        "Newton's second law of motion",
        "Newton's third law of motion"
      ],
      "subtopics": [],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Force has magnitude as well as direction",
        "Net force is zero for constant velocity; change in velocity caused by force",
        "SI unit of force",
        "Application to everyday life events"
      ]
    }
